data.description:
## Why

An OSS-practice comparison (pydantic, home-assistant, pandas, scikit-learn, dmd) confirmed
that running `pre-commit run --all-files` in CI and skipping the branch guard with
`SKIP=no-commit-to-branch` is idiomatic — NOT an anti-pattern (kept as-is). The one genuine
divergence it surfaced is a **redundant double-execution**: in the `lint + type-check` job,
`make lint` and `make typecheck` each run TWICE — once as their own named steps, and again
when `pre-commit run --all-files` re-invokes the local `lint` (=`make lint`) and `typecheck`
(=`make typecheck`) hooks. Prevailing OSS practice runs each linter ONCE (pandas/scikit-learn
route each check through a single mechanism). It is de-risked here (both paths share the same
Makefile target, so no config drift) but it is wasted CI work.

## What

Skip the `lint` and `typecheck` hooks in the CI `pre-commit run --all-files` invocation in
BOTH workflows, so the pre-commit step runs only the hooks it UNIQUELY adds
(check-yaml/toml/merge-conflict/added-large-files) and `make lint`/`make typecheck` run exactly
once each as their existing named direct steps. The hooks stay in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
unchanged, so a local `git commit` still runs lint+typecheck; only the redundant CI re-run is
dropped. This preserves every current guarantee (lint/typecheck still gate CI via the direct
steps; hygiene hooks still gate via pre-commit) while removing the double run.

## Scope

- `.github/workflows/test.yml` — change the pre-commit step `env: SKIP:` from
  `no-commit-to-branch` to `no-commit-to-branch,lint,typecheck`.
- `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` — same change to the mirrored pre-commit step.
- `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` — extend the parity guard so the double-run cannot
  silently return (assert the CI pre-commit step skips `lint` and `typecheck` in both files).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] In `.github/workflows/test.yml`, the `pre-commit run --all-files` step's `SKIP` env
      includes `lint` and `typecheck` (as well as `no-commit-to-branch`), verifiable by
      inspecting the step's `env:` block.
- [ ] In `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, the mirrored `pre-commit run --all-files` step
      applies the identical `SKIP` set.
- [ ] The `lint` and `typecheck` hooks remain PRESENT and unchanged in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
      (a local `git commit` still runs them), verifiable by diff.
- [ ] `make lint` and `make typecheck` remain as their own named gating steps in both workflows
      (each linter still runs exactly once in CI), verifiable by inspecting the job steps.
- [ ] A parity-test assertion in `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` fails the build if the
      CI pre-commit step stops skipping `lint`/`typecheck` (regression guard against the
      double-run returning). Proven RED→GREEN.
- [ ] `make lint` and `make typecheck` are green locally.
data.title: CI: trim redundant double-run of make lint / make typecheck (SKIP them in the pre-commit --all-files step)
